column1: Compiled vs Interpreted - GCC (Gnu Compiler Collection) is a compiled C implementation
column2: True Being compiled / interpreted is not tied to a specific programming language, but rather to the particular implementation of the language that you use.

column1: The main characteristics of object-oriented programming languages are?
True/False Activation records | The static offset of a variable can be computed at compile time.
column2: Correct — (True) it would not be possible to use the stack.

column1: True/False Activation records | The names of local variables are generally stored in activation records.
column2: (False) The names themselves are not stored — only the memory.

column1: True/False Activation records | Generally, the memory for all local variables of a function is allocated in its activation record.
column2: (False) Complicated, essentially memory for 'local variables' can also be stored on heap.

column1: True/False Activation records | They are only created on the run-time stack for recursive functions.
column2: (False) Activation records are created for all function calls (or scope changes).

column1: For activation records, the following holds: 1: Static chain pointers are used in C/C++/Java. 2:An activation records contains memory for local variables, but not formal parameters. 3:The dynamic chain pointer is only used for implementing dynamic scoping. 4: None of the above
